dimensions.name: Conceptual Understanding
dimensions.summary: Depth of mental model for how Claude Code works.
dimensions.definition: How well the practitioner understands the underlying architecture of Claude Code — context windows, tool use, model behavior, and system constraints.
dimensions.whyItMatters: Without accurate mental models, practitioners cannot predict tool behavior, diagnose failures, or make informed decisions about prompting strategy.
assessmentQuestions: Can you explain how context window limits affect multi-file tasks?
Do you understand the difference between tool use and direct generation?
Can you describe what happens when Claude Code executes a bash command?

dimensions.name: Task Framing
dimensions.summary: Ability to decompose and communicate tasks effectively.
dimensions.definition: The skill of breaking down work into well-scoped prompts with clear context, constraints, and success criteria that Claude Code can act on effectively.
dimensions.whyItMatters: Task framing is the primary lever for output quality. Poor framing leads to wasted iterations and incorrect results regardless of tool capability.
assessmentQuestions: Do you provide context about the codebase before asking for changes?
Can you decompose a large feature into sequential prompts?
Do you specify constraints and edge cases upfront?

dimensions.name: Verification Discipline
dimensions.definition: The practice of systematically reviewing, testing, and validating code produced by Claude Code before accepting it.
dimensions.whyItMatters: AI-generated code can be subtly wrong. Without verification discipline, bugs accumulate silently and trust erodes over time.
assessmentQuestions: Do you review every diff before accepting?
Do you run tests after each significant change?
Do you verify that generated code handles edge cases?

dimensions.name: Context Management
dimensions.summary: Skill in managing information flow to and from the model.
dimensions.definition: The ability to control what information Claude Code has access to, including file context, conversation history, and persistent configuration.
dimensions.whyItMatters: Context is the model's working memory. Managing it well improves output quality and reduces token waste. Managing it poorly leads to hallucination and drift.
assessmentQuestions: Do you use CLAUDE.md to persist project-level context?
Can you manage context window limits across long sessions?
Do you know when to start a fresh conversation vs. continue?

dimensions.name: Environment Design
dimensions.summary: Configuration of persistent tooling and project infrastructure.
dimensions.definition: The practice of configuring CLAUDE.md files, hooks, MCP servers, permissions, and project structure so that Claude Code operates more effectively by default.
dimensions.whyItMatters: A well-designed environment reduces repetitive prompting, enforces standards automatically, and makes Claude Code more productive for everyone on the team.
assessmentQuestions: Have you configured CLAUDE.md for your projects?
Do you use hooks to enforce pre-commit checks?
Have you set up MCP servers for external tool access?

dimensions.name: Workflow Scaling
dimensions.summary: Ability to orchestrate multi-agent and automated workflows.
dimensions.definition: The skill of designing workflows where multiple Claude Code instances collaborate, tasks are parallelized, and AI coding integrates into CI/CD pipelines.
dimensions.whyItMatters: Scaling beyond single-session usage is where AI coding delivers order-of-magnitude productivity gains. Without workflow design, gains plateau at individual task speed.
assessmentQuestions: Can you run multiple Claude Code agents on parallel tasks?
Have you integrated Claude Code into CI/CD pipelines?
Do you use headless/non-interactive mode for automation?

dimensions.name: Safety and Governance
dimensions.summary: Practices for safe, auditable, and governed AI code generation.
dimensions.definition: The discipline of ensuring that AI-generated code is safe, reviewed, compliant with organizational standards, and auditable in production.
dimensions.whyItMatters: As AI coding scales, the risk surface grows. Without governance, organizations face compliance failures, security vulnerabilities, and unauditable code changes.
assessmentQuestions: Do you review AI-generated code for security vulnerabilities?
Is there an audit trail for AI-generated changes?
Do you have policies for what AI agents can and cannot do?
